foundation.title: Autolyse
foundation.body: Combine flour and water until no dry flour remains, then cover and rest for 20-30 minutes to hydrate the dough.

foundation.title: Incorporate Ingredients
foundation.body: Add yeast and sugar first, then oil if used, and add salt last so it does not directly inhibit yeast activity.

foundation.title: Knead
foundation.body: Knead by hand for about 10 minutes or mixer for 5-7 minutes until smooth, elastic, and able to pass the windowpane test.

sameDay: Plan on 4 hours bulk rise and 4 hours balled proof.
If the dough gets overly bubbly or flat, bake it immediately.
coldFerment: Let dough sit at room temp for about 45 minutes before refrigerating.
Neapolitan dough should come out 3-4 hours before baking.
New York dough should come out about 2 hours before baking.
Aim for roughly 65F dough temperature before stretching.

ovenSurfaceNotes.title: Baking Steel
ovenSurfaceNotes.body: Preheat for 1 hour at max temperature for the strongest bottom heat and oven spring.

ovenSurfaceNotes.title: Baking Stone
ovenSurfaceNotes.body: Use the middle rack and give the stone about 20 minutes to recover between pizzas.

ovenSurfaceNotes.title: Home Oven Under 525F
ovenSurfaceNotes.body: Keep sugar in the dough so the crust browns before it dries out.

troubleshooting: Dough will not stretch: it is likely too cold or too tight, so cover it and wait 20 minutes.
Dough is too sticky: lightly oil your hands instead of continuing to add flour.
Bottom is soggy: the baking surface likely was not preheated long enough.

ovenAdjustments.label: Low-temp home oven
ovenAdjustments.proTip: Extended bake times dry out dough; the extra hydration and sugar are essential for a tender, browned crust.

ovenAdjustments.label: Standard pizza range
ovenAdjustments.proTip: The sweet spot for NY Style. A baking steel is highly recommended here for maximum 'oven spring'.

ovenAdjustments.label: High-heat pizza oven
ovenAdjustments.proTip: Sugar will burn instantly at these temps. Keep the dough lean and the hydration lower to avoid a 'gum line'.

neapolitan.name: Neapolitan
neapolitan.tagline: Airy, soft dough for high-heat ovens.

fermentationOptions.label: 8 hours (Same Day)
fermentationOptions.instruction: The 'Direct Method': Mix and knead until the windowpane test is passed. Bulk ferment for 4 hours, ball, and let proof for the final 4 hours at room temp. Use lukewarm water to ensure the yeast 'wakes up' quickly.
steps: Use warm water (90F-95F) to activate yeast quickly.
Combine flour and water, then rest 20 minutes for autolyse.
Add IDY yeast and mix thoroughly, then add salt.
Knead for 8-10 minutes until smooth and the dough passes the windowpane test.
Bulk rise in a covered bowl at room temperature for 4 hours.
Divide into portions and tension into tight balls to trap gas.
Let the balls proof in individual containers for 4 hours at room temperature.
Stretch and bake once the balls have relaxed and grown slightly.

fermentationOptions.label: 24 hours cold
fermentationOptions.instruction: Mix and knead, then let the mass rest at room temperature for 30 minutes to kickstart fermentation before moving to the fridge. Ball the dough at least 6 hours before baking.
steps: Use cool room-temperature water (65F-70F).
Combine flour and water, then rest 20 minutes.
Add IDY yeast and mix, then add salt.
Knead for 8-10 minutes until smooth and elastic.
Let the dough mass sit at room temperature for 30-60 minutes to kickstart fermentation.
Place the bulk mass in the fridge for 18-20 hours.
Remove from the fridge 4 hours before baking, divide into balls, and let them proof at room temperature.

fermentationOptions.label: 48 hours cold
fermentationOptions.instruction: Long cold development: Use cool water to keep the dough temperature low during kneading. Pull from the fridge 3 hours before baking to 'temper'—the dough should be 65F internally before stretching.
steps: Use cool water (60F-65F).
Combine flour and water, then rest 20 minutes.
Add IDY yeast and mix, then add salt.
Knead about 10 minutes until the windowpane test is successful.
Immediately divide the dough into balls and place them in oiled containers.
Cold ferment in the fridge for 48 hours.
Remove from the fridge about 3.5 hours before baking and wait until relaxed before stretching.

fermentationOptions.label: 72 hours cold
fermentationOptions.instruction: Maximum flavor: Enzymes have fully broken down starches into sugars. Handle very gently when balling to avoid degassing the structure. Requires a high-sensitivity scale for yeast measurement.
steps: Use cold water around 60F to prevent over-activity.
Combine flour and water, then rest 20 minutes.
Add IDY yeast and mix, then add salt.
Knead until smooth, but avoid over-kneading.
Immediately divide into balls and place them in the coldest part of the fridge.
Cold ferment for 72 hours.
Remove from the fridge 4 hours before baking and handle very gently to avoid popping air bubbles.

workflow: Bulk rise as one mass for the first 60% of fermentation.
Ball into 250g-280g portions with strong surface tension.
When stretching, push air from the center outward and avoid pressing the rim.

ny.name: New York
ny.tagline: Chewy, foldable dough for home ovens.

fermentationOptions.label: 8 hours (Same Day)
fermentationOptions.instruction: High-activity mix: Sugar and warm water will cause rapid growth. Bulk rise until doubled, ball immediately, and bake once the balls are relaxed and spread slightly.
steps: Use warm water around 95F.
Combine flour, water, and sugar, then rest 20 minutes.
Add IDY yeast, then add oil and salt.
Knead vigorously for 10 minutes to build strong gluten.
Bulk rise at room temperature until doubled, about 4 hours.
Divide and tension into balls.
Final-proof at room temperature for 4 hours until relaxed and gassy.

fermentationOptions.label: 24 hours cold
fermentationOptions.instruction: Standard NY Deli method: After kneading, ball immediately and place in oiled containers in the fridge. This creates a more uniform crumb structure.
steps: Use room-temperature water around 65F.
Combine flour, water, and sugar, then rest 20 minutes.
Add IDY yeast, oil, and salt.
Knead until very smooth and able to pass the windowpane test.
Divide and ball immediately after kneading.
Place in oiled containers and refrigerate for 24 hours.
Remove from the fridge 2 hours before baking so the gluten can relax.

fermentationOptions.label: 48 hours cold
fermentationOptions.instruction: The 'Sweet Spot': 48 hours is the ideal balance of gluten relaxation and flavor development. Remove from the fridge 2 hours before stretching. If the dough snaps back, let it rest longer.
steps: Use cool water around 60F.
Combine flour, water, and sugar, then rest 20 minutes.
Add IDY yeast, oil, and salt.
Knead about 10 minutes until tacky but strong.
Divide and ball immediately before moving to cold storage.
Cold ferment for 48 hours for optimal flavor development.
Remove from the fridge 2 hours before baking. If the dough resists stretching, rest another 30 minutes.

fermentationOptions.label: 72 hours cold
fermentationOptions.instruction: Elite flavor: The dough will be very extensible (stretchy). Use plenty of flour on your peel to prevent sticking. The crust will brown deeply and smell like toasted bread.
steps: Use cold water around 60F.
Combine flour, water, and sugar, then rest 20 minutes.
Add IDY yeast, oil, and salt.
Knead until the surface is silky.
Divide and ball immediately, using well-oiled containers.
Cold ferment for 72 hours.
Remove from the fridge 2 hours before baking and use plenty of flour on the peel.

workflow: Ball soon after mixing rather than holding a long bulk mass.
Use a knuckle stretch to open the dough thin and wide.
